guild.name: Wave Improvement Cord! | Loot & Surge Routes & Tips & Tricks

messages.content:
# Double Movement:
- Best settings for using double movement:

- It is not necessary to use 3rd Party remapping software today (Keys2XInput/WootingRemapper) because the remapper is external and may be buggier and less responsive than the native configuration inside the game settings.

- Videos on more double movement settings:
  - https://youtu.be/0ASjS6tCxDQ
  - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1UQt1qvN6cc

** **
# Sliding:
- Sliding and sprinting continuously is better than sprinting:

You get high momentum maintained sprinting and sliding, yet you consume less energy over time. This is called the "muddy sprint" as it was exploited and popularized during `Chapter 4 Season 3: Wild` when it was used in the mud. This was where sliding made you travel faster than before (until it was nerfed towards the end of the season)

- You can get into boxes while sliding but it has to be right timing, place a cone and edit it like a stair or just straight up place a stair facing the other way. Then make the wall extremely low so u can break it with a single shot from a SMG, AR or even a shotgun. If you slide and shoot the wall as you are about to hit the wall, You will enter the box and then you have freedom to do anything you like.

- Videos to watch about sliding:
  - https://youtu.be/8TURT7t-aiw
  - https://youtu.be/DGwKiDvrMJw
  - https://youtu.be/FINwgdRTVpE
  - https://youtu.be/xiKg0CoJJjg
  - https://youtu.be/agevytXqTx4

** **
# Mantling:
- When an emeny buildfights you and 90s higher than you, they will ramp towards you from above.  You can sidejump onto their ramp and follow them.

- If an enemy is buildfighting you and placed a cone whilst ramping towards you too, you can mantle on top of it, sprint, and build ramps for height again.

- Use SPACE or JUMP to mantle instead of run forward to mantle. You may accidentally mantle onto a higher level if you are running into a raised floor or other build. With jump function, you can mantle standing still to, especially mantling through a half floor above you then resetting it to lay the full floor down.

- If taking descent TOO quickly: Rotate ramp backwards and hold place. When it places on the ground, hold mantle, and you should be free from damage. Useful if you have no ANTI-FALL DAMAGE item or have ran out of charges on it or have nothing to save you.

- If you are out of mats in endgame or during a fight: You can use mantling to get up to someone's builds and try to get a refresh or if you are out of mats you can mantle up to old builds to survive longer and get more points.

- Reasons why you should try to sprint and jump higher onto another layer in front of you instead of mantling:
  - You may experience a pullout delay for your weapon upon mantling so you are left prone to damage.
  - You cannot build instantly to protect yourself from damage or play defensively in general. 
  - The enemy might counter your mantle by placing a wall at your position during mantle to void your ascent and may lead you to fall damage with failed mantle attempt or losing height advantage and risk losing the fight.

- Videos to watch about mantling
  - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rj5mP5NBnac
  - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eGxK4otkLKc

** **
# New Movement [Wall Kicks/Ledge Jumps/Wall Scrambles/Roll Landing]
- Wall Kicks:
Sprint alongside a wall, jump, then jump again against the wall

- Ledge Jumps: 
Sprint and then jump before an edge

- Wall Scrambles:
Find a high wall, run towards it, jump, then jump again

- Roll Landing:
From a higher spot, jump and then hold jump before you land

- Videos to watch about the new movement:
  - https://youtube.com/shorts/7Yb462iCwM8?si=ff3WXvKuhMcO7qEQ
  - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0EgSMl0wa_o

** **
# More Movement Videos:
